chunk_summary: This section provides concise caregiver counseling scripts for THR, WASH, and WIFS, tailored for Anganwadi, OPD, and school settings to improve pediatric nutrition and health outcomes.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of brief, targeted counseling scripts for caregivers in pediatric nutrition.
Learn the specific THR, WASH, and WIFS scripts and their application contexts.
Identify measurable behavior targets for follow-up in Anganwadi and CMAM visits.
Incorporate counseling scripts as auditable interventions in clinical practice.
key_facts: THR script emphasizes feeding the child first and setting daily targets to prevent nutrition loss.
WASH script highlights handwashing, safe water use, and zinc supplementation for 10–14 days during diarrhea episodes.
WIFS script advises taking tablets after food, managing side effects like black stools, and monitoring for severe anemia symptoms.
Scripts are designed to be brief (15–20 seconds) and linked to observable behaviors for follow-up.
Counseling scripts should be documented as part of the prescription for auditable interventions.
reference_quote: “Diarrhea steals nutrition. Handwash before feeding, use safe water, and start ORS early. Give zinc for 10–14 days even if diarrhea stops.”
document_key_topics: Caregiver counseling for THR, WASH, and WIFS
Integration of scripts into clinical practice
